rulings.Case Title: STONERIDGE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. DEANZA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 1. CROSS-DEFENDANT KWAN HENMI ARCHITECTURE & PLANNING, INC.'S Motion To Bifurcate IS DENIED. THE PARTIES ARE DIRECTED TO REQUEST THE PRESIDING JUDGE FOR ASSIGNMENT TO A SINGLE JUDGE FOR ALL PURPOSES. IN THIS WAY, THE MANAGEMENT OF THESE CASES CAN BE EFFECTIVELY ADDRESSED. =(302/PJM/JF)

rulings.Case Title: STONERIDGE HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION VS. DEANZA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 1. DEFENDANT SADDLEBACK HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF IN THAT DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT PLAINTIFF CANNOT PROVE A CONTINUING NUISANCE OR THAT PLAINTIFF CONSENTED TO THE CONDITIONS; NOR HAS DEFENDANT ESTABLISHED UNDISPUTED FACTS NECESSARY TO ESTABLISH AN EASEMENT. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT ON THE ISSUE OF A CONTINUING NUISANCE AS SET FORTH IN THE HERZOG DECLARATION. NO FOUNDATION HAS BEEN LAID AS TO THE ENABLING DOCUMENT, THEREBY NEGATING A MATERIAL FACT UPON WHICH DEFENDANT RELIES. THERE ARE TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT REGARING WHEN ROCKS FELL SO AS TO PRECLUDE A FINDING OF AN EASEMENT. EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS TO BE RULED UPON AT THE HEARING. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN SUVAL et al VS. MARK O'FLYNN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion And Ntc Of Motion (Anti-Slapp Motion)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANT MARK O'FLYNN'S Motion (Anti-Slapp Motion). MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. PARAGRAPH 18 OF THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES CONDUCT THAT IS NOT PROTECTED AND IT IS THAT CONDUCT WHICH IS THE GRAVAMEN OF PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN SUVAL et al VS. MARK O'FLYNN et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 15. DEFENDANT MARK O'FLYNN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED, TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. DEFENDANT'S ALLEGED FAILURE TO INSTALL A BATH RAIL IS AN ALLEGED FAILURE TO "REPAIR" OR "SERVICE", NOT A FAILURE TO "MODIFY". THE SEVENTH AND EIGHTH CAUSES OF ACTION ALLEGE THAT DEFENDANT TRIED TO FORCE PLAINTIFF FROM HER HOME BY ILLEGALLY DISPLACING PLAINTIFF AND RENDERING THE PREMISE INACCESSIBLE WITH KNOWLEDGE OF PLAINTIFF'S DISABILITY. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: BEAU GIANNINI et al VS. JOHN RYERSEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Counsel
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 11. PLAINTIFF BEAU GIANNINI, UNOVIV L.L.C., D.B.A. PRIME TIME MARKETING, AND FOX'S Motion To Disqualify Counsel IS DENIED. THE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE BEFORE THE COURT IS INSUFFICIENT TO DISQUALIFY COUNSEL. THE COURT HAS NOT TAKEN NOTICE OF THE RELATED CASE BECAUSE THAT CASE SHOULD HAVE BEEN A PART OF THE MOVING PARTY'S MOVING PAPERS SO AS TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY NOTICE AND THE OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND. =(302/PJM/KD)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HAMMAN VS. JEAN CLAUDE COURNOYER et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, MAY 4, 2007, LINE 13. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL HAMMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED IN PART; GRANTED IN PART. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF FAILED TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF ON THE DEFENSE OF RETALIATORY EVICTION AND THE DEFENSE OF BREACH OF COVENANT OF QUIET ENJOYMENT. THERE IS A COMMON LAW DEFENSE OF RETALIATION. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON HABITABILITY, NON-COMPLIANCE WITH THE RENT ORDINANCE. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT THE LEASE WAS FOR A RESIDENTIAL PURPOSE. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR LACHES. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT THAT PLAINTIFF ENGAGED IN UNREASONABLE DELAY. GRANT MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION FOR WAIVER/ESTOPPEL BASED ON SECTION 12 OF THE LEASE. =(302/PJM/AP)
